_metadata.description: Canonical hint strings keyed by error.code for Steelbore CLIs. Every hint is a runnable command (paste-test passes). Substitute <tool> with the actual tool name (ferrocast, caliper, etc.).

NOT_FOUND.context: Resource the agent asked for does not exist. Hint points to the listing command for that resource type.

INVALID_ARGUMENT.context: Argument did not parse or violated a constraint. Hint points to the schema sub-command so the agent can read the canonical parameter spec.

USAGE_ERROR.context: Command-level usage error (missing required arg, conflicting flags, etc.). Hint points to --help. Prefer INVALID_ARGUMENT with schema hint when the issue is a specific argument.

PERMISSION_DENIED.context: Lacks credentials or filesystem permission. Hint depends on subtype.

CONFLICT.context: Resource already exists or is in conflicting state. Default hint inspects the existing resource; --force hint is for legitimate override cases. Prefer the inspect hint to avoid destructive recovery.

RATE_LIMITED.context: Hit a rate limit. Include retry_after_seconds in the error envelope; hint chains sleep + retry.

INTERNAL_ERROR.context: Tool itself failed. Hint points to the diagnostics command. NEVER leave hint empty even for internal errors.

MISSING_DEPENDENCY.context: Required external tool not in PATH. Per Steelbore standard, prefer Nix package manager. Use --with-check hint when agent should query the dependency list first.

TIMEOUT.context: Operation exceeded its time budget. Hint suggests increasing the timeout. Caller may instead want to retry with smaller scope.

NETWORK_ERROR.context: Network operation failed. Diagnostics command checks connectivity, DNS, proxy.

VALIDATION_FAILED.context: Input data failed schema validation (distinct from INVALID_ARGUMENT which is parse-time). Hint runs the validator standalone for full error report.

QUOTA_EXCEEDED.context: User or org quota exceeded. Hint shows current quota state.

DEPRECATED.context: Used a deprecated command or flag. The schema output's replaced_by field tells the agent the new form.

UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION.context: Operation not supported in this configuration / version / platform. Hint shows the capability manifest so the agent learns what IS supported.

DRY_RUN_REQUIRED.context: Destructive operation attempted without --dry-run preview in agent mode. Hint runs the dry-run; agent reviews plan before retrying with --yes.

CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED.context: Destructive operation in non-TTY mode without --yes / --force. Hint adds the consent flag. The agent harness logs the consent.
